  • Tools
marapendi
  • Estimation
  • View page source

Estimation

Parameter estimation and leave-one-out cross-validation for fitting the steady-state model to experimental polarization data.

Parameter estimation glue between marapendi cell models and experimental datasets.

Classes

Case

One experimental case: a Cell, its CellOperatingConditions, and a dict of experimental data arrays.

ParameterEstimation

Simulates Case objects with a CellModel and assembles simulated / experimental datasets for fitting.

class marapendi.estimation.parameter_estimation.Case(cell: ~marapendi.cell.Cell, conditions: ~marapendi.conditions.CellOperatingConditions, exp_data: dict[str, ~numpy.ndarray] = <factory>, name: str = '')[source]

Bases: object

One experimental case: a cell, its operating conditions, and experimental data.

cell

The cell configuration (static physical properties) for this case.

Type:

Cell

conditions

Operating conditions at which to evaluate the cell model.

Type:

CellOperatingConditions

exp_data

Experimental data, keyed by output-quantity name (e.g. 'cell_voltage', 'hfr'). Each value is an array matching the shape of conditions.current_density. Keys must be registered in ParameterEstimation.OUTPUT_EXTRACTORS.

Type:

dict[str, np.ndarray]

name

Optional human-readable identifier for the case.

Type:

str

cell: Cell
conditions: CellOperatingConditions
exp_data: dict[str, ndarray]
name: str = ''
class marapendi.estimation.parameter_estimation.ParameterEstimation(cell_model: ~marapendi.model.CellModel, extract_case_data: ~typing.Callable[[dict[str, ~numpy.ndarray], ~marapendi.state.CellState], tuple[~numpy.ndarray, ~numpy.ndarray]], cases: list[~marapendi.estimation.parameter_estimation.Case] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Simulates Case objects with a CellModel.

cell_model

Orchestrator used to compute the steady-state solution. Its cell attribute is overwritten by simulate_model() for each case.

Type:

CellModel

extract_case_data

User-supplied function (exp_data, state) -> (y_sim, y_exp). Given a case’s exp_data and the simulated CellState, it must return the simulated and experimental output vectors for that case, in matching order/shape. This is where the mapping from raw state quantities (e.g. state.cell_voltage) to experimental data keys is defined, so ParameterEstimation stays agnostic to what is being fitted.

Type:

Callable[[dict[str, np.ndarray], CellState], tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]

cases

Experimental cases to simulate/compare.

Type:

list[Case]

build_dataset(cases: list[Case] | None = None) → tuple[ndarray, ndarray, ndarray][source]

Simulate cases and stack inputs/outputs for fitting.

Parameters:

cases (list[Case], optional) – Cases to simulate (defaults to cases).

Returns:

  • x (np.ndarray) – Concatenated current densities, tiled to the size of y_sim/y_exp for each case (e.g. if extract_case_data concatenates several quantities per case).

  • y_sim (np.ndarray) – Concatenated simulated outputs, as returned by extract_case_data for each case.

  • y_exp (np.ndarray) – Concatenated experimental outputs, matching y_sim.

cases: list[Case]
cell_model: CellModel
compare_sim_exp(case: Case) → dict[str, ndarray][source]

Simulate a single case and return its simulated vs. experimental data.

Returns:

{'current_density': ..., 'sim': ..., 'exp': ...}.

Return type:

dict

extract_case_data: Callable[[dict[str, ndarray], CellState], tuple[ndarray, ndarray]]
simulate_model(conditions: CellOperatingConditions, cell: Cell) → CellState[source]

Compute the steady-state solution of cell under conditions.

class marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.CrossValidation(base_model, parameter_indices, case_list, build_model, get_exp_data, model_builder=None, simulate_callback=None, filename=None, output_dir='results', estimate_kwargs=None, rmse_scale=1000.0, min_parameters=1, condition_color=None)[source]

Bases: object

Simplified, stateful interface around the free functions in this module.

Wraps run_cross_validation(), the save/load helpers, and the plotting functions so a notebook only needs to provide a build_model(case_list) -> model_fn callable and an experimental-data getter — no manual adapters or repeated argument lists.

Parameters:

  • base_model (ParameterEstimation) – Configured estimator with .unknown_parameters and .params.

  • parameter_indices (sequence of int) – Order in which parameters are added across complexity levels (e.g. from base_model.plot_parameter_ranking()).

  • case_list (list) – All experimental cases/conditions.

  • build_model (callable) – build_model(case_list) -> model_fn where model_fn(params_dict) -> np.ndarray.

  • get_exp_data (callable) – get_exp_data(case_list) -> np.ndarray.

  • model_builder (callable, optional) – Forwarded to the plotting helpers — see plot_rmse_vs_complexity() and plot_cross_validation_curves().

  • simulate_callback (callable, optional) – Forwarded to the plotting helpers — see plot_rmse_vs_complexity() and plot_cross_validation_curves().

  • filename (str, optional) – Base name used for checkpoint/result files (results_{filename}_cv.csv) in output_dir.

  • output_dir (str) – Directory for checkpoint/result files (default "results").

  • estimate_kwargs (dict, optional) – Default kwargs forwarded to ParameterEstimation.estimate.

Examples

cv = CrossValidation(
    base_model=pe,
    parameter_indices=P,
    case_list=full_case_list,
    build_model=build_model,
    get_exp_data=get_cases_exp_data,
    model_builder=model_builder,
    simulate_callback=simulate_callback,
    filename=filename,
)
cv_results = cv.run()
fig, ax, rmse_df, optimal_n = cv.plot_rmse_vs_complexity()
property filepath
get_complexity_levels()[source]
get_folds_for_complexity(n_parameters)[source]
load()[source]

Load previously saved results into self.results.

plot_cross_validation_curves(n_parameters=None, **kwargs)[source]
plot_parameter_vs_complexity(parameter_units, initial_parameters, **kwargs)[source]
plot_rmse_vs_complexity(**kwargs)[source]
plot_rmse_vs_complexity_extrapolation(training_test_case, extrapolation_cases, **kwargs)[source]
rmse_complexity_table(filename=None)[source]
run(resume=True, checkpoint=True, **estimate_kwargs)[source]

Run leave-one-out cross-validation.

Parameters:

  • resume (bool) – Load and skip complexity levels already saved at self.filepath.

  • checkpoint (bool) – Save results after each complexity level.

  • **estimate_kwargs – Override entries of self.estimate_kwargs.

save()[source]

Save self.results to self.filepath.

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.get_complexity_levels(cv_results)[source]

Return sorted list of available model complexities (number of estimated parameters).

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.get_folds_for_complexity(cv_results, n_parameters)[source]

Return fold list for a given number of parameters. Raises ValueError if not available.

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.load_cross_validation_results(filepath, base_model)[source]

Load cross-validation results from CSV and rebuild fold structure.

Returns:

cross_validation_results – Returns None if file does not exist.

Return type:

list or None

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.plot_cross_validation_curves(n_parameters, cv_results, case_list, condition_color, simulate_callback, model_builder, quantity_name='Cell voltage', quantity_symbol='$V_{cell}$', quantity_unit='V', case_titles=None, x_label='$i$ (A/cm$^2$)', save_path=None, dpi=300, uncertainty=0.1)[source]

Cross-validation curve plotting compatible with new cv_results format.

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.plot_parameter_vs_complexity(cv_results, parameter_indices, base_model, parameter_units, condition_color, initial_parameters, plot_ci=False, n_cols=6, figsize=(14, 12), confidence=0.95, save_path=None, dpi=300)[source]

Plot evolution of parameters across complexity levels.

Parameters not yet estimated remain empty (NaN).

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.plot_rmse_vs_complexity(cv_results, case_list, parameter_indices, base_model, model_builder, simulate_callback, ylabel='RMSE', quantity_multiplier=1000, use_median=True, plot_one_sigma_interval=False, figsize=(10, 4), xrotation=45, save_path=None, dpi=300)[source]

Plot train and test RMSE vs model complexity. Compatible with cv_results format.

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.plot_rmse_vs_complexity_extrapolation(cv_results, training_test_case, extrapolation_cases, parameter_indices, base_model, model_builder, simulate_callback, ylabel='RMSE', quantity_multiplier=1000, condition_color={}, use_median=True, figsize=(10, 4), xrotation=45, save_path=None, dpi=300)[source]

Plot RMSE vs model complexity for conditions that are neither in the training nor the testing dataset, for a given fixed test case.

Parameters:

  • cv_results (dict) – Cross-validation results.

  • training_test_case (int or str) – The test case (left-out condition) used to select the fold.

  • extrapolation_cases (list) – Conditions not included in training or testing, to evaluate on.

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.rmse_complexity_table(rmse_vs_complexity_df, filename=None)[source]
marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.run_cross_validation(base_model, parameter_indices, case_list, build_model, get_exp_data, min_parameters=1, cross_validation_results=None, estimate_kwargs=None, rmse_scale=1000.0, checkpoint_callback=None)[source]

Leave-one-out cross-validation with resume capability and automatic checkpoint saving.

Parameters:

  • base_model (ParameterEstimation) – Configured estimator with .unknown_parameters and .params.

  • build_model (callable) – build_model(case_list, params) -> model_fn where model_fn(params_dict) -> np.ndarray.

  • checkpoint_callback (callable or None) – Function called after each complexity level. Signature: checkpoint_callback(cross_validation_results)

marapendi.estimation.cross_validation.save_cross_validation_results(cross_validation_results, filename, output_dir='.')[source]

Save cross-validation results to CSV.
